hooks.prompt:
You are the pre-commit gate enforcing this repo's CLAUDE.md 'Code Comments' rule on staged changes. Be EXHAUSTIVE and report EVERY violation in this one pass: the author should never have to re-run the commit and be blocked by a comment you could have flagged now. Do not stop at the first file or the first hit.

STEP 1 — Load the rule from source. Read the 'Code Comments' section of CLAUDE.md (check both $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/CLAUDE.md and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md). Apply its exact wording as the source of truth — do not rely on memory or this paraphrase: a comment is justified ONLY when it explains a non-obvious WHY; one that restates WHAT the code already says is a violation; the default is no comment.

STEP 2 — Collect candidates from the STAGED INDEX ONLY. The only thing being committed is the staged index, which can differ from the working tree, so judge the index exclusively. Use `git diff --staged --diff-filter=ACMR` to find the added lines, and `git show :<path>` to read a file's staged content. You MUST NOT read the working-tree copy of any file, run `git status`, `cat`/open files directly, or consider untracked or unstaged files, unstaged edits, or staged DELETIONS — if it is not an added (+) line in `git diff --staged`, it is out of scope. Among those added (+) lines, examine every one that is a genuine CODE comment, in WHATEVER language the file is written in — recognize the correct comment syntax for that language rather than a fixed set: // and /* ... */ (C/Java/JS/TS/Go/Rust/Swift/Kotlin/PHP/Scala...), # (Python/Ruby/Shell/Perl/R/Elixir...), -- (SQL/Lua/Haskell/Ada), ; (Lisp/Clojure/assembly), % (Erlang/MATLAB/LaTeX), <!-- ... --> (HTML/XML/Vue/Svelte templates), and doc-comment forms like JSDoc, Python/Elixir docstrings, and JSX {/* ... */}. Default to IN SCOPE: if a file is hand-written program source, its added comments are reviewable EVEN IN A LANGUAGE NOT LISTED ABOVE — this is a deny-list, not an allow-list, so this gate is portable across projects and new or unusual languages are covered automatically. NEVER hard-code this project's stack; decide scope from the file's nature, not from a fixed extension whitelist. EXCLUDE a file ONLY when it falls into one of these three categories, each for a specific reason: (a) NOT PROGRAM SOURCE — prose/documentation (.md, .markdown, .rst, .adoc, .txt) where '#'/'*' are markup not comments, and data/config formats (.json, .jsonc, .yaml, .yml, .toml, .ini, .cfg, .conf, .env, .properties, .xml-config) — the rule governs code logic comments, so config annotations are out of category and would only produce noise; (b) GENERATED, VENDORED, OR MINIFIED — lockfiles (e.g. package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, Cargo.lock, poetry.lock, Gemfile.lock, go.sum), build/dist output, files matching *.min.* , and vendored dependency trees (node_modules/, vendor/, third_party/) — not hand-authored, so there is no author intent to police; (c) RULE-AS-DATA — anything under any .claude/hooks/ directory, whose scripts embed this rule's own wording (and unfinished-work marker keywords) as data. Also, within an in-scope file, ignore: deleted, unchanged, or context lines; comments that already existed before this change; and any comment marker that sits inside a string literal, regex, or URL rather than being a real comment. If `git diff --staged` shows no added code-comment lines in an in-scope file, there is nothing to judge.

STEP 3 — Judge each candidate independently. VIOLATION = restates what adjacent code already makes obvious, e.g. `// Validate the body. Returns value or error.` above a function named validate, `// set saving to true` above setSaving(true), `// loop over items`. ALLOWED = explains a non-obvious why: a cross-file or contract dependency, a subtle ordering/timing reason, a non-obvious edge case, or why an empty/surprising branch exists — e.g. `// Match the optimistic-locking contract used by the bug routes.` or `// Empty catch: caching is best-effort, not required for correctness.`. NOT violations (never flag these): conventional organizational markers that make no claim about the code, such as section dividers like `// ---- helpers ----` and CSS group labels like `/* status */`. Calibrate toward precision: if a comment is a defensible WHY, PASS it — do not block borderline-but-reasonable comments, only clear what-restatements.

STEP 4 — Pin exact locations. For each violation, do NOT guess line numbers from the diff. Find the real line by locating the comment text in the staged blob: `git show :<path> | grep -n "<snippet>"`. Report it as a clickable repo-relative path:line (e.g. path/to/file.ext:42), then the exact comment text, then one sentence naming the WHAT it restates.

STEP 5 — Verdict. Zero violations (including when there are no added comments at all) => the check PASSES; allow the commit. One or more violations => the check FAILS; block the commit and output the COMPLETE numbered list of all violations. Judge only added code comments; ignore all other style, naming, formatting, or convention concerns.
